next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 16

Two days after Sable walked out of the farmhouse, the Scour still was not under them. From the farmhouse the basin had been about two hard days on foot by the direct southern road if they were willing to be seen. Vel had spent those same two days taking them east through dead irrigation cuts and fallen relay posts, then south in crooked pieces, then east again whenever the open road showed fresh dust or mirror-flash. They had not gone backward, but they had barely bought half a day's southing. The direct line belonged to whoever the Red Reserve already had between the farmhouse and the basin. The loop bought them cover, one intact pump, three walls still standing, and proof Sable had not been trying to scare them for sport. It had not bought them time. They were still about two days from the basin because they'd spent two days not going straight at it.

Yesterday, belly-down in a dry canal with reeds turned to wire around their faces, Vel had watched two riders take the direct road south. Not Margins enforcers. Their spacing was too neat, their horses too quiet, and neither man looked around with the loose greed of somebody hoping to extort travelers. One had stopped at an old relay frame and flashed a polished signal plate toward the basin. Ten minutes later another flash answered from farther down. After that she stopped thinking in terms of warnings and started thinking in terms of coverage.

The detour had cost them anyway. Kem spent seven levels in the stupid little bites that passed for caution when you still had levels left: one for light in a collapsed...
open_hooks_to_carry: Sable alone at 238, having dismissed her team; six weeks of transformation
Sable's encounter with a former Hoarded defector carrying Stillness damage — speech slowing, eyes drifting, the condition portable and visible outside the Vault
Three-way confrontation in a Margins farmhouse — Sable arrives to a broken alliance; dark comedy of bureaucratic speech patterns meeting raw reality
Kem tells Sable about Lord Aldraste's Stillness; Sable recognizes it from the defector she just met
Sable's choice: she cannot return him to what she now understands as slow death
Red Reserve has a permanent Scour posting — classified assignment, agents stationed for years — the Hoarded have been guarding the threshold longer than Vel realized
Sable heads south, not back to the Vault — driven by a personal question: if zero is a door, then everything she served was protecting people from freedom

payoffs_to_land.description: Stillness encountered through both Kem's testimony and the Margins defector — Sable recognizes it from Council members she's worked with for decades, and the double confirmation breaks her institutional loyalty
The Red Reserve deployment is both new (Council authorization for Kem retrieval) and old (permanent Scour garrison) — the escalation reframes the Hoarded as active suppressors

prev_tail_excerpt:
...ll there, whole and waiting. But the next day's terms had shape now. Not trust. Terms.

He held out his hand for the journal without having planned to. Vel looked at it. For a moment he thought she would refuse, not out of secrecy but out of habit so old it would take more than one night to break. Instead she rose, came around the fire, and set the book down on the ground between their bedrolls.

"Don't lose it," she said.

"I wasn't planning to."

"People plan badly."

It was the longest thing she had said to him since the house. It was also, in its bent and meager form, almost ordinary.

She went back to her side of the fire and lay down with her boots still on.

Dren watched the journal where it lay between them. "That's cozy," he said.

"Go to sleep," Vel said.

"Trying."

Below them, a dog barked once and was answered by another. Somebody coughed behind one of the west-side houses. The wall held. The crack kept its own counsel in the dark.

Kem sat with the journal a long minute before he opened it. Maren's hand was neater than Vel's, less crowded, as if the world had once seemed willing to fit where it was put. Vel's notes took over where that confidence ended. Same route. Different pressure. Different person.

He closed the book and kept it beside his pack.

The pack stayed buckled because unbuckling it would have meant claiming something either way. Staying. Leaving. Morning. He was not ready to call any of those done.

But he left the journal where he could reach it, and when the east side gave one small settling clatter in the dark, all three of them woke at once.
secondary_character_beats: Sable's decision is a personal identity crisis, not a heroic defection — she is not joining anyone, she is failing in a way she cannot make herself regret
The Margins defector with Stillness damage makes the condition visceral and present-tense — not backstory but evidence

setups_to_plant.description: Sable's personal question about zero — if the threshold is real, the entire Hoarded philosophy was suppressing freedom — drives her south and toward the Scour, seeding her essential contribution in Ch 18
The Red Reserve's permanent Scour posting reveals the Hoarded have been suppressing threshold knowledge for years — the garrison knows the terrain and the mechanism

state_deltas_required: Sable's identity crisis meeting the fractured alliance; the revelation of the Hoarded's active threshold suppression; present-tense Stillness through the Margins defector

Sable becomes a person rather than a force; the Hoarded revealed as active suppressors of the threshold; Sable's personal question drives her south; the Red Reserve threat activates with the permanent Scour posting
